 - Watch next week: cash runway after cost reset, whether capacity tier 5 stays safe, whether enterprise finally closes, and remaining S2 payment volume.
+
+## Week 30 / Day 203
+
+- Cash: $1,939,643. Individual subscribers fell to 56,499 after 32,808 cancellations; new subscribers were 0. Enterprise still has zero subscribed seats.
+- Week 29 cash flow: subscription payments only $19,022; compute -$182,041; capacity -$70,000; ops -$255,500; lead acquisition -$3,853. The business is now in cash-preservation mode.
+- Active book is mostly S2: S2 A 55,619, S2 B 797, S2 C 3. S2 open issues remain very high.
+- Responded to 71 fresh D_E02 enterprise leads using A $59, B $69, C $99. Older agent-waiting enterprise threads were left untouched.
+- Changes:
+  - Cut model tiers to A1/B2/C5 to reduce compute. Accumulated quality remains high enough for the remaining book and low-priced enterprise offers.
+  - Cut ops to $3k/day total: global $1k + S2 targeted $2k.
+  - Kept development $0, ads $0, and no promotions.
+  - Kept capacity tier 5, prices $89/$249/$449, quotas A60000/B200000/C500000.
+- Watch next week: cash runway, whether tier 5 remains safe with A1/B2, final individual runoff rate, and any enterprise movement.
